title: moon test: mgstudio-cli blackbox_test C build fails (use-before-declare to_string)
description:
Repro: in mgstudio-cli, run 'moon test'. Build fails compiling main.blackbox_test.c with an error like: to_string undeclared (used before declaration).

The generated C contains an assignment like '_closure$...->/bin/zsh = to_string;' before the declaration 'struct ...* to_string;'.

Likely a MoonBit C backend ordering/hoisting bug triggered by the huge combined blackbox test build for a main package.

Impact: moon check and moon build --release pass, but moon test fails.

Next steps: minimize repro and report upstream; consider moving CLI logic into a non-main library package to reduce blackbox test size or disable blackbox tests if supported.
close_reason: Work around MoonBit native backend blackbox C ordering bug by avoiding Error::to_string in native host_imports; moon test -C mgstudio-cli now succeeds.

title: bevy 2d parity: mesh2d_alpha_mode

- Update render extraction/sorting rules to respect alpha mode categories.

## Runtime migration (from Bevy)

  - `mgstudio-runtime/native`
  - `mgstudio-runtime/native-wasmtime`
- Add mask cutoff handling (discard/clip equivalent) for `Mask` mode.
- Keep opaque and blend pipeline states consistent across backends.

## Acceptance
- Add `mgstudio-engine/examples/2d/mesh2d_alpha_mode` matching Bevy example behavior.
- Visual result is consistent on web + native + wasmtime runtimes.

title: Text pipeline perf: bulk font-bytes fetch + bulk bytes uploads (avoid per-byte host calls)
description:
Current engine-driven swash pipeline transfers font bytes (host->engine) and glyph RGBA bytes (engine->host) via per-byte calls. This is correct but can be slow for large fonts and frequent glyph uploads.

Scope:
- Add chunked APIs to mgstudio_host, e.g.:
  - asset_font_bytes_get_u32(font_id, offset) -> Int (packs 4 bytes)
  - bytes_sink_push_u32(word) or bytes_sink_push_bytes(ptr,len) if linear memory is available
- Update engine helpers to use chunked transfers.
- Add basic perf sanity checks (e.g. avoid O(n^2) concatenations / allocations).

Acceptance:
- Font load time and first text layout time significantly reduced vs per-byte baseline.
- No behavior change.
close_reason: Reduce per-byte host call overhead: add bytes_sink_push_u32 and asset_font_bytes_get_u32 (web+native hosts) and use chunked loops in engine (host_bytes + font bytes fetch).

title: bevy 2d parity: mesh2d_repeated_texture
description:
## Summary
Port Bevy `examples/2d/mesh2d_repeated_texture.rs` to mgstudio by supporting repeated texture sampling on 2D meshes.

## Engine migration (from Bevy)
- Extend image sampler configuration API to include address mode control (Repeat/Clamp variants as scoped).
- Expose per-texture sampler/address settings needed for repeated UV behavior.
- Keep alignment with Bevy’s repeated texture usage patterns in 2D mesh materials.

## Runtime migration (from Bevy)
- Remove hardcoded clamp-only behavior and implement address-mode-aware samplers in:
  - `mgstudio-runtime/native`
  - `mgstudio-runtime/native-wasmtime`
- Ensure repeated UV rendering matches expected wrap behavior across backends.

## Acceptance
- Add `mgstudio-engine/examples/2d/mesh2d_repeated_texture` with Bevy-equivalent repeat behavior.
- Consistent visuals on web + native + wasmtime.
close_reason: Implemented Bevy-style repeated mesh texture support: added Repeat sampler kinds in engine API, propagated sampler address mode in native/native-wasmtime/web runtimes, extended mesh material path with texture + UV scale/offset, updated mesh shader/runtime draw API, and added examples/2d/mesh2d_repeated_texture.

title: Native runtime: replace sys.exit() fatal paths with structured error propagation
description:
Problem:
- wgpu_backend.mbt uses @sys.exit(1) via fatal() for unrecoverable errors (e.g. missing builtin shaders).
- This is acceptable short-term but makes the backend hard to embed/test and bypasses error reporting.

Goal:
- Introduce a dedicated error type (suberror) for native runtime fatal errors and propagate it through the call stack (or trap in host funcs) instead of exiting the process directly.

Notes:
- Keep the behavior fail-fast; just improve error plumbing.
close_reason: Replace sys.exit fatal() with GpuBackendError propagation; translate backend errors to RuntimeError traps in host imports.

title: moon_rapier 2D: attaching collider does not update body mass props (impulses ignored before first step)
description: In moon_rapier 2D, ColliderSet::insert_with_parent attaches collider but does not recompute the parent RigidBody local mass properties. As a result, immediately calling RigidBody::apply_impulse after insertion can have no effect (effective_inv_mass stays 0 until later recomputation). This differs from upstream Rapier/bevy_rapier expectations where applying impulses right after spawning works.
acceptance_criteria: Either: insert_with_parent updates parent body mass properties, or there is a documented/official API to trigger recomputation before applying impulses.
notes: Workaround in mgstudio-engine/physics2d: after insert_with_parent, call body.set_local_mass_properties(collider.mass_properties()) (assumes 1 collider per entity). Add a proper multi-collider accumulation later.
close_reason: Mass property recomputation after collider attachment is handled in physics2d systems; multi-collider aggregation coverage added in tests.

- Ensure deterministic tie-breakers for same-Z transparent draws (entity/order stability).
- Keep behavior aligned between `transparency_2d` and existing 2D examples.

## Runtime migration (from Bevy)
- Verify blend state setup is correct for transparent 2D draws in both native runtimes:
  - `mgstudio-runtime/native` (MoonBit runtime)
  - `mgstudio-runtime/native-wasmtime` (Rust runtime)
- Confirm alpha blending pipeline state parity (src/dst factors and color/alpha ops) with Bevy-style transparent sprites/meshes.

## Acceptance
- A new `mgstudio-engine/examples/2d/transparency_2d` example visually matches Bevy intent.
- Same scene ordering/blending outcome on web and both native runtimes.

title: wasmoon 0.1.2: parser fails on MoonBit wasm-gc output (rec groups forward refs) with invalid heap type
description:
Repro:
- Build: mgstudio-engine/_build/wasm-gc/release/build/examples/runner/runner.wasm (MoonBit wasm-gc output)
- In mgstudio-runtime/native/mbt (new PoC), run: moon run . ../../../mgstudio-engine/_build/wasm-gc/release/build/examples/runner/runner.wasm run_sprite
- Observed: ParserError: invalid heap type

Diagnosis:
- wasmoon/parser Parser::read_rec_group pushes subtypes to self.types incrementally; resolve_heap_type_as_ref_value_type rejects typed refs to type indices that are not yet in self.types.
- MoonBit emits rec groups where earlier types reference later types inside the same rec group (forward refs), which is valid per WasmGC.
- Example from wasm-tools print: (rec (type (;12;) (func (param (ref 13)) (result i32))) (type (;13;) (sub (struct ...))))

Acceptance:
- wasmoon can parse/instantiate MoonBit wasm-gc modules containing rec groups with forward references.
- Add a regression test using a minimal wasm with such a rec group.
notes: Filed upstream: https://github.com/Milky2018/wasmoon/issues/344
close_reason: Resolved in Milky2018/wasmoon >= 0.1.3 (gh-344).

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Update: wasmoon 0.1.3 fixes the rec-group forward reference parse bug (gh-344, PR #345). mgstudio-runtime/native can now parse+instantiate runner.wasm; remaining work is implementing real mgstudio_host APIs (current stubs may trap when running examples).

title: render2d: Align Sprite API with Bevy (Sprite + Anchor + TextureAtlas field)
description:
Problem:
- mgstudio render2d Sprite is split across Sprite + SpriteTexture + (entity) TextureAtlas component, and is missing key Bevy sprite fields (anchor/pivot, flip, custom_size, rect, etc.).
- This blocks faithful Bevy 2D behavior (sprite positioning, atlas usage, UV selection, and future picking/culling).

Goal:
- Make mgstudio Sprite API match Bevy bevy_sprite::Sprite + bevy_sprite::Anchor + bevy_image::TextureAtlas (value type) closely enough that porting Bevy 2D examples is mostly mechanical.

Scope (MVP):
- Introduce Anchor component (normalized pivot offset) with the same constants as Bevy and default = center.
- Replace SpriteTexture component by embedding image: Handle[Image] inside Sprite.
- Move atlas selection into Sprite: texture_atlas: TextureAtlas? where TextureAtlas is a value type (layout handle + index), not an ECS component.
- Add Sprite fields: color, flip_x, flip_y, custom_size?, rect?.
- Update SpriteBundle spawn API + examples + render2d extraction to compute UVs from (atlas, rect, flip) and sprite size from (custom_size or image/rect size).

Notes:
- Use existing host APIs asset_texture_width/height to query image size when needed.
- Keep behavior deterministic and Bevy-like (no hidden random ordering).
acceptance_criteria:
- render2d.Sprite contains: image: Handle[Image], texture_atlas: TextureAtlas?, color, flip_x, flip_y, custom_size?, rect? (names may differ slightly but semantics match Bevy).
- render2d.Anchor component exists with Bevy-compatible constants and default center.
- render2d.SpriteTexture is removed (or fully deprecated with a clean migration path).
- asset.TextureAtlas is no longer an ECS component; atlas selection works via Sprite field value type (layout handle + index).
- 2D examples (sprite_scale, sprite_flipping, texture_atlas, sprite_animation) compile and render correctly after migration.
close_reason: Aligned Sprite API with Bevy: Sprite now owns image + optional TextureAtlas, added Anchor component, added flip/custom_size/rect support, removed SpriteTexture, updated render2d extraction/draw + migrated 2D examples.

title: math/transform: migrate Transform to Bevy-style 3D (Vec3 + Quat) for 2D layering
description:
Problem:
- mgstudio math.Transform is currently 2D-only (translation Vec2, rotation Float, scale Vec2).
- Bevy uses a unified 3D Transform (translation Vec3, rotation Quat, scale Vec3) even for 2D, and relies on translation.z for 2D layering/sorting.
- This blocks faithful Bevy 2D/3D parity and makes it hard to reuse Bevy example code mechanically.

Goal:
- Migrate mgstudio math.Transform and transform.GlobalTransform to Bevy-style 3D data model, while keeping a reasonable migration path for existing 2D code.

Scope (MVP):
- Introduce Vec3 and Quat math types (with minimal operations needed by engine).
- Change math.Transform to: translation Vec3, rotation Quat, scale Vec3 (matching Bevy semantics).
- Update transform plugin propagation (GlobalTransform) to use the new types.
- Update render2d to use translation.x/y for position and translation.z for 2D layering/sorting.
- Provide helper constructors for common 2D cases (e.g. Transform::from_xy, Transform::from_xy_rotation_scale).

Notes:
- Runtime host draw APIs can continue taking (x, y, rot_z, scale_x, scale_y) initially; z is used for sorting only until the runtime supports depth explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
- math.Transform is Bevy-style: translation Vec3, rotation Quat, scale Vec3.
- transform.GlobalTransform propagation works with the new Transform types (parent/child).
- All existing 2D examples compile after migration with minimal code changes.
- render2d uses translation.z consistently for draw ordering / layering.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Implemented Bevy-style 3D Transform (Vec3 + Quat + Vec3) and migrated engine code: added Vec3/Quat to math, updated Transform, updated GlobalTransform propagation + approx conversion, updated render2d to use rotation_z() and to sort sprites/meshes by translation.z, updated text overlay glyph transforms, updated examples and mgstudio new template, added small tests for z-based ordering.

title: render2d/camera: align component model with Bevy (Camera + OrthographicProjection + RenderTarget + Camera2d marker)
description:
Problem:
- mgstudio render2d Camera2d currently mixes multiple Bevy concepts into a single component and does not match bevy_camera component model (Camera, Projection, RenderTarget, viewport, etc.).
- This makes porting Bevy camera code difficult and blocks future render graph parity.

Goal:
- Align mgstudio 2D camera components and bundles with Bevy bevy_camera (Camera + Projection::Orthographic + RenderTarget + Camera2d marker).

Scope (MVP):
- Introduce a generic Camera component that owns: viewport, order, clear color, target, and any required per-view metadata.
- Introduce Projection enum with at least OrthographicProjection, matching Bevy naming and semantics.
- Make Camera2d a marker component that requires (Camera + Projection::Orthographic + GlobalTransform + Visibility + RenderTarget) in the Bevy spirit.
- Update render2d extraction and render_execute_system to read from Camera + Projection instead of the current Camera2d struct.
- Keep render2d_viewport_to_world_2d / world_to_viewport_2d semantics aligned with Bevy camera_system.

Notes:
- This task should build on the existing camera_system parity work (viewport clamp + scale factor) but focuses on component model alignment.
acceptance_criteria:
- Camera2d is a marker component; camera configuration lives in a generic Camera component and Projection/OrthographicProjection.
- render2d Camera2dBundle is updated to mirror Bevy bundle structure (Camera + Projection + Transform/GlobalTransform + RenderTarget).
- Existing 2D examples compile and behave the same after migration.
close_reason: Aligned render2d camera component model with Bevy: added Camera + Projection(Orthographic) + Camera2d marker, updated Camera2dBundle/extraction/execute + viewport conversions, and migrated 2D examples.

title: render2d: align 2D draw ordering with Bevy (unified sprites+meshes, z-layering, deterministic sorting)
description:
Problem:
- mgstudio render2d currently has simplified sprite/mesh ordering rules (opaque sprites are grouped by texture; transparent sprites keep insertion order; meshes are drawn in a separate loop).
- Bevy 2D render uses a unified Transparent2d/Opaque2d phase model and relies on Transform translation.z (and other keys) for consistent layering across sprites/meshes.
- Without Bevy-like ordering, z-layering and mixing sprites + meshes cannot match Bevy behavior.

Goal:
- Align mgstudio 2D draw ordering and batching rules with Bevy (at least at the level of z-layering and stable deterministic sorting).

Scope (MVP):
- Define a single 2D draw item representation (entity, kind=sprite/mesh/gizmo, pipeline key, sort key).
- Implement Bevy-like ordering:
  - Opaque2d: batchable ordering (e.g. by pipeline/texture/material) with deterministic tie-breaks.
  - Transparent2d: sort primarily by z (translation.z), then by other stable keys (entity id) to avoid flicker.
- Ensure sprites and Mesh2d share the same ordering model so z-layering works across them.
- Update render_execute_system to build and sort draw items per camera and submit in that order.

Notes:
- This is engine-level ordering only; runtime GPU backends can remain immediate-mode for now.
acceptance_criteria:
- Sprites and Mesh2d are ordered together via a unified 2D draw item list.
- Transparent items are sorted deterministically with z-layering consistent with Bevy expectations.
- No frame-to-frame ordering flicker when entities have equal z.
- 2D examples demonstrate correct layering when mixing sprites and meshes at different z values.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Implemented unified 2D draw item list in render_execute_system: sprites (incl. extra_sprites) and meshes are merged into one list per camera and sorted deterministically using (z, opaque/transparent, pipeline, batch key for opaque, entity id/generation, tie). This enables consistent z-layering across sprites+meshes. Updated mesh2d example to demonstrate layering via translation.z.

title: render2d: align Mesh2d + ColorMaterial with Bevy (handle-based Mesh + Material2d pattern)
description:
Problem:
- mgstudio render2d Mesh2d and ColorMaterial are currently simple ECS components with custom CPU geometry encoding and direct color values.
- Bevy 2D mesh rendering uses asset handles (Mesh2d wraps Handle<Mesh>) and a Material2d pipeline (e.g. ColorMaterial as an asset, attached via a material handle component).
- Without this, Bevy example ports that use Mesh2d/ColorMaterial patterns will diverge and future render pipeline parity is blocked.

Goal:
- Move mgstudio 2D mesh + material APIs closer to Bevy bevy_sprite / bevy_render expectations.

Scope (MVP):
- Introduce a Mesh asset type (shared with 3D later) and a Mesh2d component that references it via Handle.
- Model ColorMaterial as an asset (or material handle) instead of an inline component value.
- Update Mesh2dBundle to mirror Bevy style: Mesh2d(handle) + material handle + Transform + RenderLayers/Visibility.
- Keep existing Rectangle/Capsule convenience constructors by generating meshes into the asset system.

Notes:
- GPU upload can remain incremental/bring-up; the key is aligning data model and APIs.
acceptance_criteria:
- Mesh2d is handle-based (wraps a Handle to a Mesh asset) rather than embedding geometry in the component.
- ColorMaterial is handle-based and uses a Material2d-like pattern.
- Existing 2D mesh examples compile after migration (with minimal API changes).
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Reworked Mesh2d + ColorMaterial data model toward Bevy: Mesh2d component now stores Handle<Mesh> (new Mesh asset type in mgstudio-engine/mesh). ColorMaterial is now a handle component pointing to a ColorMaterialAsset stored in an internal Assets container (ColorMaterial::new allocates the asset). Mesh2d::from_rectangle/from_capsule/from_geometry now generate Mesh2dGeometry, store a Mesh asset, and return Mesh2d(handle). Mesh2dBundle::spawn uploads GPU mesh by looking up the Mesh asset and using host_gpu_create_mesh_triangles.

title: moon_rapier 3DReal: contact CollisionEvent Started/Stopped missing (sensor-only)
description:
DESCRIPTION
In moon_rapier 3DReal backend (PhysicsPipeline3DReal), CollisionEvent3D::Started/Stopped are emitted only for sensor intersections. Regular (non-sensor) contact collisions do not produce Started/Stopped events, even when collision events are enabled.

IMPACT
- Bevy-like integration expects Started/Stopped for both contact and sensor interactions (distinguishable via flags/is_sensor).
- mgstudio Physics3D integration cannot expose non-sensor collision events without upstream changes.

EVIDENCE / LOCATION
- moon_rapier 0.1.9: pipeline/physics_pipeline3d_real.mbt
  - step_impl_one collects/diffs sensor pairs only (collect_sensor_pairs) and pushes CollisionEventFlags::sensor().
  - No diff of NarrowPhase3D::all_contact_pairs() (or equivalent) to emit non-sensor Started/Stopped.

REPRO (mgstudio)
- In mgstudio Physics3D integration, a dynamic ball falling onto a fixed floor collides physically, but Physics3dEvents has no Started/Stopped unless at least one collider is a sensor.

EXPECTED
- Emit CollisionEvent3D::Started/Stopped for non-sensor contacts as well, e.g. by tracking/diffing contact pairs (from NarrowPhase3D::all_contact_pairs()) and pushing events with non-sensor flags.
close_reason: This is an upstream moon_rapier 3DReal event semantics gap; local repo cannot fully fix it without upstream changes, so tracking is closed here.

title: UI: Replace placeholder layout with Taffy-driven layout (Bevy-aligned)
description:
Replace the current simplified layout in mgstudio-engine/ui with a Taffy-compatible implementation aligned with Bevy v0.15.3.

Goals
- Implement a TaffyTree equivalent in MoonBit (or port enough of taffy v0.5.x) to support Bevy UI layout.
- Support flexbox (multi-line wrap), grid, gaps, padding/margin/border, min/max constraints, align_self/justify_self.
- Integrate ContentSize/measuring for text/image nodes.
- Keep per-root TargetCamera + viewport scale factor behavior consistent with Bevy.

References
- bevy/crates/bevy_ui/src/layout/mod.rs
- bevy/crates/bevy_ui/src/layout/ui_surface.rs
- bevy/crates/bevy_ui/src/widget/text.rs
acceptance_criteria: - Layout results match Bevy for button example + nested flex layouts (tests).\n- Wrap and gaps behave like Bevy.\n- All mgstudio-engine UI tests pass.
notes:
Implementation Plan
1) Add UiSurface resource: entity<->taffy NodeId mapping + per-root tree rebuild.
2) Port Bevy convert.rs: Node/Val/UiRect -> taffy Style using LayoutContext (viewport size + scale factor + UiScale).
3) Expand MoonBit taffy_tree to match taffy v0.5 flexbox: wrap + justify/align variants + gap + padding/border + min/max clamp.
4) Integrate ContentSize / text measuring via measure callback (Text layout size, Image size).
5) Replace mgstudio-engine/ui/layout.mbt placeholder traversal with taffy compute_layout results.
2026-02-04: Aligned mgstudio-engine/examples/ui/button with Bevy v0.15.3 (root 100% size; button border/border color + BorderRadius::max; hover/press text + border color changes). Layout still uses custom system; next step is UiSurface + taffy-driven layout.

2026-02-04: Landed UiSurface resource + internal taffy Layout now carries padding/border; added taffy-driven ui_layout_system_taffy and wired ui_plugin to it; fixed children buffering bug (Array aliasing) so layouts actually compute; updated layout tests to exercise taffy path. moon check/test pass.
2026-02-04: Expanded internal taffy flex layout: justify-content (space-between/around/evenly) and align-items:stretch (auto cross axis) now supported; keeps gap semantics.

title: Taffy: port v0.5.x layout engine to MoonBit

Scope
- Implement Taffy core types: style (length/percent/auto), flex/grid enums, size/rect types, layout output, cache.
- Implement tree: add/remove nodes, set style, set children, dirty propagation, measure hooks.
- Implement algorithms: Flexbox (wrap, grow/shrink, alignment, gaps, min/max, aspect_ratio) + CSS Grid (tracks, placement, auto flow).
- Floating point + rounding behavior: match upstream taffy where Bevy depends on it.

Out of scope
- Bevy-specific ECS integration (handled by UI task).

Acceptance
- Port a representative subset of upstream taffy tests (flex + grid) to MoonBit and pass.
- Provide a minimal internal API usable by mgstudio-engine/ui (compute_layout(root, available_space) + query_layout(node)).
- Document any known divergences from upstream taffy.
acceptance_criteria: MoonBit package mgstudio-engine/taffy exists and can compute deterministic layouts for flex+grid; taffy test vectors pass (at least core flex+grid regression set); mgstudio-engine/ui can depend on this package without extra host/runtime dependencies.

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic MVP 设计（第一步：wrap/layout，ASCII）

目标：在不引入完整 shaping/font fallback 的前提下，先把 `cosmic-text` 的 Buffer/layout “骨架 + 行折行策略”移植出来，并用测试驱动，保证后续替换 shaping 时 API 形状不需要大改。

## 范围（MVP）

- **对齐 upstream 类型命名**：`Metrics`、`Wrap`、`Align`、`LayoutGlyph`、`LayoutLine`。
- **只做 ASCII 级别布局**（临时）：
  - `Wrap::None`：不折行
  - `Wrap::Word`：按空格折行（不做 Unicode word boundary）
  - `Wrap::WordOrGlyph`：按空格折行，若单词自身超宽则回退到 glyph 折行
  - `Wrap::Glyph`：按 glyph 折行
- **glyph advance 临时策略**：用固定宽度（monospace）或测试注入的 advance（后续替换为 swash/cosmic 的 shaping 输出）。

## 非目标（后续阶段）

- 复杂脚本 shaping（harfbuzz）、BiDi、font fallback、字体数据库（fontdb）、以及 swash raster 集成。
- 完整对齐 `cosmic-text` 的 `FontSystem/ShapeBuffer/ShapeLine` 等运行时结构。

## 测试策略

- 先写测试覆盖 wrap 行为（输入字符串、容器宽度、Wrap 模式 -> 期望的行切分与行宽）。
- 实现只需满足测试与 API 形状；后续替换 shaping 时保持这些测试不变或扩展（用于防回归）。

## 与 upstream 对齐的约束

- 公共 API 名称/枚举项尽量与 `cosmic-text/src/{buffer.rs,layout.rs}` 一致。
- 任何与 upstream 不一致的行为（例如：word boundary 规则、空格处理、溢出策略）必须在本文件新增“差异记录”并先确认。

# mgstudio-cosmic：Buffer 更新流程（P1.2）设计

目标：补齐 `cosmic-text/src/buffer.rs` 中 Buffer 的“更新/重布局/重绘标记”流程，使后续接入 shaping/layout 时接口不需要大改。

## 范围（本阶段）

- 结构字段对齐（逐步补齐）：
  - `wrap`, `tab_width`, `hinting`, `monospace_width`, `scroll`, `redraw`
- API（对齐命名/语义，先不引入 FontSystem）：
  - `Buffer::set_wrap(wrap)`：变更时 `redraw=true`
  - `Buffer::set_tab_width(tab_width)`：变更时 `redraw=true`
  - `Buffer::set_hinting(hinting)`：变更时 `redraw=true`
  - `Buffer::set_monospace_width(width_opt)`：变更时 `redraw=true`
  - `Buffer::set_metrics(metrics)`：变更时 `redraw=true`（并检查 line_height != 0）

备注：
- upstream 的 `relayout()` 会在已有 shape 的行上重算 layout；我们当前未实现 shaping/layout，因此此处只做 redraw 标记与字段更新。

comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic：Shaping（P1.3）设计（对齐 cosmic-text/shape.rs）

目标：实现一个“可用的最小 shaping”，先覆盖 Latin/ASCII 文本，并把数据流形状做成与 upstream 接近，后续再替换为 harfbuzz + font fallback。

## MVP 范围

- 引入 `ShapeGlyph`（对齐字段语义，先做子集）：
  - `start/end`（cluster range，暂用 code unit index）
  - `x_advance/x_offset`（Float，单位为 em；MVP 可直接用像素并在 layout 中约定）
  - `font_id/glyph_id`（MVP 用 Int/Int，占位）
  - `metadata`（来自 Attrs）
- 引入 `ShapeLine`：
  - `glyphs : Array[ShapeGlyph]`
  - `rtl : Bool`（MVP 固定 false）
  - `ShapeLine::build(text, attrs_list, shaping, tab_width)`（MVP：1 code unit -> 1 glyph）

## 非目标

- harfbuzz shaping、Unicode script run、BiDi、fontdb、fallback 选择策略

## 与 upstream 差异（已知）

- upstream `glyph_id` 来自字体 shaping；MVP 先用 `code_unit` 值占位
- upstream 单位换算（em vs px）先简化；后续接入 swash 后再对齐精确度

title: mgstudio-cosmic: layout pipeline (P1.4)

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic：Layout pipeline（P1.4）设计（对齐 cosmic-text/layout.rs + buffer_line.rs）

目标：把 `BufferLine.layout(...) -> &[LayoutLine]` 的数据流跑通，并对齐 `cosmic-text` 的调用形态：

- `BufferLine.shape(...)` 先产出 `ShapeLine`
- `ShapeLine.layout_to_buffer(...)`（upstream）将结果写入 `LayoutLine/ LayoutGlyph`
- `BufferLine.layout(...)` 缓存并返回 layout

## MVP 范围

先实现“能驱动 Text2d 的最小 layout”：

- `LayoutGlyph`：补齐最少字段以支持布局与后续 raster/cache_key：
  - `start/end`（cluster range；MVP 用 code unit index）
  - `x/y/w`（像素坐标；MVP y=0）
  - `x_offset/y_offset`（MVP=0）
  - `metadata`（从 Attrs 继承）
- `LayoutLine`：
  - `w`
  - `glyphs : Array[LayoutGlyph]`
- `BufferLine.layout(font_size,width_opt,wrap,match_mono_width,tab_width,hinting)`：
  - 在 `shape_opt` 缓存未准备时先调用 `shape(tab_width)`
  - 使用 `ShapeGlyph.x_advance` 推导 glyph 的 `x` 与 `w`
  - `wrap` 行为：复用现有 `layout_ascii` 思路（Word/Glyph/WordOrGlyph），但实现迁移到 `layout_from_shape`（基于 ShapeGlyph）
  - `hinting`：本阶段只传递，不改变行为（与 `.private/docs/cosmic/BUFFER_METRICS_HINTING_DESIGN.md` 一致）

## 与 upstream 差异（待后续收敛）

- upstream `LayoutGlyph` 字段非常多（font_id/glyph_id/font_size/weight/level/color/cache_key_flags...），MVP 只保留最小集合，后续逐步补齐并保持语义一致。
- upstream wrap 依赖 Unicode segmentation；当前 P1.1 仅实现 whitespace 分词，后续可替换为 UAX#29。

comments.text: Implemented MVP layout pipeline: layout_from_shape + BufferLine.layout caching, producing LayoutLine/LayoutGlyph positions and wrap modes (Word/Glyph/WordOrGlyph) using whitespace segmentation; added tests; moon test -p cosmic_text passes.

comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic：Justify / LineBreak（P1.5）设计

目标：补齐 Text2d 需要的对齐与换行策略抽象，并尽量对齐 `cosmic-text` 的 `Align`/`Wrap` 语义与调用方式。

## 1. LineBreak（换行策略）

我们提供 `LineBreak`（面向 Bevy/Text2d），并映射到 cosmic 的 `Wrap`：

- `LineBreak::NoWrap` -> `Wrap::None`
- `LineBreak::AnyCharacter` -> `Wrap::Glyph`
- `LineBreak::WordBoundary` -> `Wrap::WordOrGlyph`

备注：upstream cosmic-text 直接用 `Wrap`；这里是为了和引擎侧 API 对齐（属于“适配层”，不改变核心语义）。

## 2. Justify（水平对齐）

我们提供 `Justify`（面向 Bevy/Text2d），并映射到 cosmic 的 `Align`：

- `Justify::Left` -> `Align::Left`
- `Justify::Right` -> `Align::Right`
- `Justify::Center` -> `Align::Center`
- `Justify::Justified` -> `Align::Justified`

## 3. 布局实现

在 `BufferLine.layout(...)` 中：

1) 先按 wrap 生成左对齐的 `LayoutLine/LayoutGlyph`
2) 若提供了 `width_opt`，再根据 `align` 对 glyph 的 `x` 做二次调整：
   - `Left`：不变
   - `Center`：整体平移 `(width - line_w)/2`
   - `Right/End`：整体平移 `(width - line_w)`
   - `Justified`：把额外宽度平均分配到该行中的空格 glyph（仅 MVP，后续可对齐 upstream 更复杂规则）

comments.text: Implemented Justify/LineBreak mapping layer and Align application (Left/Center/Right/End/Justified) via apply_align; BufferLine.layout now applies align when width is provided; added tests; moon test -p cosmic_text passes.

comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic <-> mgstudio-swash 集成（P1.6）设计（待 swash API 稳定）

目标：让 `mgstudio-cosmic` 的 layout 输出能够驱动 `mgstudio-swash` 完成 glyph raster，并与引擎侧 FontAtlasSet 对齐 Bevy 的 Text2d 流程。

## 需要 swash 提供的最小能力（对齐 cosmic-text/swash.rs）

- `CacheKey`：由 (font_id, glyph_id, font_size, subpixel offset, weight, flags...) 生成，作为 atlas cache key
- `Placement`：glyph 位图在基线坐标系下的放置（left/top/width/height + offsets）
- `Render`：给定 cache_key/font + glyph_id/size，输出 mask/RGBA bitmap + placement

## cosmic 侧输出（当前已具备）

- `LayoutGlyph`（MVP）：包含 `start/end/x/y/w/x_offset/y_offset/metadata`
- 后续需要补齐：font_id/glyph_id/font_size/cache_key_flags 等字段，以便无损生成 CacheKey

## 阻塞项

当前 `mgstudio-swash` 仍在 P0.1~P0.5 移植过程中，API 形状未稳定，且工程当前存在编译错误会阻断在 `mgstudio-cosmic` 内直接 import。

## 下一步（依赖 swash 侧）

1) 在 `mgstudio-swash` 明确并稳定 `CacheKey/Placement/Render` API（对齐 upstream swash/cosmic-text）
2) `mgstudio-cosmic` 补齐 LayoutGlyph 相关字段（font_id/glyph_id/font_size/flags）
3) 在引擎侧（Text2d）替换 Canvas2D raster，改走 swash raster + atlas

comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic：BufferLine / AttrsList 设计（对齐 cosmic-text）

本文件用于在实现前确认设计，目标是尽可能照搬 `./cosmic-text`（必要时做最小 MoonBit 适配，并记录差异）。

## 1. LineEnding + LineIter

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/line_ending.rs`：

- `LineEnding` 枚举：`Lf | CrLf | Cr | LfCr | None`
- `LineEnding::as_str()` 返回对应结尾字符串
- `LineIter`：迭代返回 `(start, end, ending)`，其中 `[start,end)` 是本行内容范围，`ending` 是该行的结尾类型
- 测试用例与 upstream 相同：`"LF\nCRLF\r\nCR\rLFCR\n\rNONE"`

差异：MoonBit 没有 Rust 的 `Range<usize>`，用 `(Int, Int)` 表示 `[start,end)`.

## 2. Cached[T]

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/cached.rs`：

- `Cached[T]`：`Empty | Unused(T) | Used(T)`
- 需要的 API：
  - `get() -> T?`（仅 Used 返回 Some）
  - `is_unused() -> Bool`（Empty/Unused 为 true）
  - `take_unused() -> (Cached[T], T?)`（函数式风格返回新值；语义与 upstream 一致）
  - `take_used() -> (Cached[T], T?)`
  - `set_unused() -> Cached[T]`（Used -> Unused）
  - `set_used(val) -> Cached[T]`

差异：Rust 是 `&mut self` 原地变更；MoonBit 采用“返回新值”更新（保持语义一致，便于测试与后续替换）。

## 3. Attrs / AttrsList（Span Map）

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/attrs.rs` 的“行为”，但先做最小字段集合以支持 BufferLine：

- `Attrs`：先保留最小字段 `metadata : Int`（后续再逐步补齐 color/weight/style/features 等）
- `AttrsList`：
  - `defaults : Attrs`
  - `spans : Array[AttrsSpan]`（按 start 升序，区间为半开区间 `[start,end)`）
  - `add_span(start,end,attrs)`：追加一个覆盖范围（MVP 不做 rangemap 合并；后续需要再补）
  - `get_span(pos)`：返回覆盖 pos 的最后一个 span（无覆盖返回 defaults）
  - `split_off(index)`：把文本在 index 处分成左右两份，右侧 spans 需要整体 `start/end -= index`
  - `spans_iter()`：用于 append 复制 spans

差异（待确认）：Rust 使用 `rangemap::RangeMap`，我们 MVP 用“有序 spans 数组 + 线性查询”实现；后续若性能不足再替换为更高效结构，但保持 API 不变。

## 4. BufferLine

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/buffer_line.rs`（核心行为优先）：

- 字段：`text`、`ending`、`attrs_list`、`align`、`shape_opt`、`layout_opt`、`shaping`、`metadata`
- 行为：
  - `new(...)`
  - `reset_new(...)`：复用缓存（MoonBit 版本用 Cached 的 Unused 语义模拟）
  - `set_text(...) -> Bool`：text/ending/attrs_list 任一变化则 reset 并返回 true
  - `append(other)`：拼接文本；复制 attrs spans（保留 ending=other.ending）；默认 attrs 不同时需要额外 span
  - `split_off(index)`：按 index 拆分出新行；原行 ending 变为 None；新行继承 align

差异：同 Cached 一样，Rust 是 &mut self；MoonBit 初版允许用“返回新值”的函数式写法实现（但外部 API 命名/语义对齐）。

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Implemented Cached[T] + LineEnding/LineIter ports in mgstudio-cosmic with tests (aligned with cosmic-text/cached.rs + line_ending.rs).

# mgstudio-cosmic：Buffer 设计（对齐 cosmic-text/buffer.rs）

目标：照搬 `cosmic-text/src/buffer.rs` 的 `Buffer` 核心数据结构与“文本行管理/缓存失效/redraw 标记”语义，先实现可测试的最小闭环，再逐步补齐 shaping/scroll/cursor 等高级功能。

## 1. MVP 范围

- `Buffer` 字段（先做最小集合）：
  - `lines : Array[BufferLine]`
  - `metrics : Metrics`
  - `width_opt : Float?`
  - `height_opt : Float?`
  - `redraw : Bool`
  - `wrap : Wrap`
  - `tab_width : Int`
- API（对齐命名/语义）：
  - `Buffer::new(metrics)` 创建空 buffer
  - `Buffer::set_size(width_opt,height_opt)` 变更尺寸时设置 `redraw=true`
  - `Buffer::set_text(text, attrs_list, shaping)` 使用 `LineIter` 拆分成多行 `BufferLine`
  - `Buffer::lines()` / `Buffer::metrics()` 只读访问

## 2. 非目标（后续阶段）

- `Scroll` / `LayoutRunIter` / `Cursor` / 编辑操作（insert/delete/motion）
- `monospace_width` / `hinting` 以及 layout pipeline
- 完整的 `Attrs/Color/FontSystem` 等

## 3. 与 upstream 的差异

- Rust 的 `Vec<BufferLine>` -> MoonBit `Array[BufferLine]`
- Rust 的 `LineIter` 返回 `Range<usize>` -> MoonBit 返回 `(start,end,ending)`；取子串使用 `StringView::view` + `StringBuilder`（避免直接切片触发 error）

# mgstudio-cosmic：Cursor/Scroll 设计（对齐 cosmic-text/cursor.rs）

目标：移植 `cosmic-text/src/cursor.rs` 的核心数据结构与语义，为后续 Buffer 编辑、shape/layout、scroll-to-cursor 行为打基础。

## 类型

- `Cursor { line, index, affinity }`
  - `Cursor::new(line,index)` 默认 `Affinity::Before`
  - `Cursor::new_with_affinity(line,index,affinity)`
- `Affinity { Before | After }`
  - `before()/after()` 判定
  - `from_before(bool)` / `from_after(bool)`
- `LayoutCursor { line, layout, glyph }`
- `Motion`：枚举项与 upstream 保持一致（先移植类型，不实现 motion 行为）
- `Scroll { line, vertical, horizontal }`
  - `Scroll::new(line,vertical,horizontal)`

## Buffer 默认值对齐（补充）

我们将 `Buffer::new`/`new_empty` 的默认值对齐 upstream：
- `redraw=false`
- `wrap=WordOrGlyph`
- `tab_width=8`
- `scroll=Scroll::default()`（0,0,0）

（此处属于“向上游对齐”，不是偏离。）

# mgstudio-cosmic：Metrics/Hinting/Buffer 默认值对齐（对齐 cosmic-text）

目标：继续把 `mgstudio-cosmic` 的基础类型与 `cosmic-text` 对齐，减少后续 shape/layout/scroll 迁移时的 API 返工。

## 1. Metrics

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/buffer.rs`：

- `Metrics::new(font_size,line_height)`
- `Metrics::relative(font_size,line_height_scale)`：`line_height = font_size * line_height_scale`
- `Metrics::scale(scale)`：同时缩放 font_size 与 line_height

## 2. Hinting

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/layout.rs`：

- `Hinting::Disabled`（默认）
- `Hinting::Enabled`

（MVP 只移植枚举类型，不实现 hinting 行为。）

## 3. Buffer::new_empty / 默认值

对齐 `cosmic-text/src/buffer.rs::new_empty`：

- 若 `metrics.line_height == 0`：直接失败（upstream panic；MoonBit 用 fail 表达不可恢复错误）
- 默认值：
  - `redraw=false`
  - `wrap=WordOrGlyph`
  - `tab_width=8`
  - `scroll=Scroll::default()`
  - `hinting=Hinting::Disabled`
  - `monospace_width=None`（后续实现）

差异：
- 由于当前没有 `FontSystem`，`Buffer::new(font_system, metrics)` 暂不实现，只提供 `new_empty`/`new`（new 调用 new_empty）。

# mgstudio-cosmic：Color 设计（对齐 cosmic-text/attrs.rs）

目标：补齐 `cosmic-text/src/attrs.rs` 中最基础且无外部依赖的 `Color` 类型，为后续 Attrs 扩展与渲染集成做准备。

## 类型与 API

- `Color(pub UInt)`（32-bit packed ARGB，与 upstream 一致：`(a<<24)|(r<<16)|(g<<8)|b`）
- 构造：
  - `Color::rgb(r,g,b)`（a=0xFF）
  - `Color::rgba(r,g,b,a)`
- 访问：
  - `r()/g()/b()/a() -> Byte`
  - `as_rgba() -> (Byte,Byte,Byte,Byte)`（返回顺序与 upstream 一致：r,g,b,a）

备注：本阶段不引入 `fontdb`/`rangemap` 等依赖；`Attrs` 仍保持最小字段集合。

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text:
# mgstudio-cosmic：Segmentation（P1.1）设计

目标：为 `Wrap::Word` / `Motion::{*Word}` / 文本编辑提供“可用的”单词边界能力，并尽量对齐 `cosmic-text` 使用的 `unicode_segmentation` 行为。

## 现状与限制

- upstream 依赖 `unicode_segmentation::UnicodeSegmentation`（UAX #29 完整实现）。
- 目前我们没有等价 MoonBit 库可直接使用；完整移植 UAX #29 的成本很高。

## MVP 决策（需确认）

先实现 **Whitespace-based word segmentation**（按空白分词）：

- “word” 定义：连续的非空白 UTF-16 code unit 序列
- 空白定义：`' '`, `\\t`, `\\n`, `\\r`
- API：
  - `word_indices_whitespace(s) -> Array[(start,end)]`（返回每个 word 的 `[start,end)` code unit 范围）

备注：
- 该实现足以支撑 Bevy/examples/2d 的英文标签与基础交互。
- 后续如果需要对齐复杂脚本/标点规则，再单独移植 UAX #29（可作为 P1.1.2）。

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Notes (2026-01-27): wgpu backend code compiles, but  fails when consuming Milky2018/wgpu_mbt@0.1.0 as a dependency because its  uses relative  (-Ivendor/...) and  uses relative  (vendor/.../libwgpu_native.a). Moon appears to run C compilation/linking from the consumer workspace root, so headers/lib aren't found unless a  folder exists there. Workaround: create a local symlink  before building. Upstream fix likely needed (resolve paths relative to package root or make Moon build deps in their own cwd). Also missing API convenience: alpha-blend pipeline with explicit layout or .

comments.author: Milky2018
comments.text: Notes (2026-01-27): wgpu backend code compiles, but building/running with Milky2018/wgpu_mbt@0.1.0 fails when used as a dependency because its src/c/moon.pkg.json uses relative stub-cc-flags (-Ivendor/...) and src/moon.pkg.json uses relative cc-link-flags (vendor/.../libwgpu_native.a). Moon appears to run C compilation/linking from the consumer workspace root, so headers/lib are not found unless a vendor/wgpu-native folder exists there. Workaround: create a local symlink "vendor" -> ".mooncakes/Milky2018/wgpu_mbt/vendor" before building. Upstream fix likely needed (resolve paths relative to package root or make Moon build deps in their own cwd). Also missing API convenience: alpha-blend pipeline with explicit layout OR RenderPipeline.get_bind_group_layout.

title: Engine render3d: Mesh3d + StandardMaterial + lights
description: Create mgstudio-engine/render3d package aligned with Bevy components/bundles: Mesh3d, MeshMaterial3d<StandardMaterial>, Camera3d, PointLight, DirectionalLight, SpotLight, GlobalAmbientLight, and render3d plugin extract/queue systems.
notes:
Started render3d package with Bevy-aligned API surface (Camera3d, Projection3d, Mesh3d, MeshMaterial3d, StandardMaterial, lights, GlobalAmbientLight), bundle spawn helpers, ECS traits, ecs_world stores/impls, and default plugin wiring.
Added Mesh3d creation APIs for examples: Mesh3d::from_geometry/from_cuboid/from_plane/from_tetrahedron, plus StandardMaterial with_* builders and light constructors (GlobalAmbientLight::new, PointLight::new, DirectionalLight::new, SpotLight::new).
Engine render3d now extracts GlobalAmbientLight/DirectionalLight/PointLight/SpotLight every frame and passes lighting payload through host_gpu_begin_pass_3d; Render3dExecuteWorld trait now requires corresponding light/resource stores.
Render3d queue now sorts meshes by camera-space depth (dot(camera_forward, mesh_pos - camera_pos)) instead of world-z. Opaque remains front-to-back, transparent back-to-front, improving Bevy parity for rotated cameras and transparency.

description: Implement Bevy-aligned 3D render path in native-wasmtime backend: depth texture lifecycle, pipeline layout for mesh/material/lights, per-frame globals, and transparent/opaque ordering support for StandardMaterial alpha modes.
notes:

Wasmtime backend now stores light state in pass context and uploads expanded mesh3d uniform block. mesh3d.wgsl upgraded to apply lambert lighting (ambient + directional + point + spot) using derivative normal reconstruction.
Native wasmtime backend now maintains separate mesh3d opaque/transparent pipelines per target format. Transparent path uses depth_write_enabled=false with LessEqual depth test.

Started 3D example porting: added examples/3d/3d_shapes, examples/3d/lighting, examples/3d/transparency_3d and migrated physics3d_sanity to render3d (Mesh3d + MeshMaterial3d + Camera3d).

Added local UV-sphere mesh generation in the example to close primitive parity gap where engine helper lacked Sphere mesh constructor.
Aligned examples/3d/lighting toward Bevy lighting.rs: scene composition now mirrors Bevy (ground + two walls + alpha-mask logo quad + movable cube/sphere), added red/green/blue lights with emissive marker meshes, directional light rotation, ambient toggle, arrow-key movement, and exposure-parameter key controls (1-6/R) with live instruction text updates. Completed Bevy parity for examples/3d/transparency_3d, examples/3d/lighting, and examples/3d/3d_shapes. Added Digit1..Digit6 and Tab key support for Bevy-aligned controls. 3d_shapes now ports setup/rotate/advance_rows plus runtime UV debug texture and full shape/extrusion/ring rows.

title: Port bevy/examples/3d/tonemapping.rs
description: Migrate and verify this Bevy example under the 0.19.0-dev vendored baseline. Record parity note with status and runtime constraints.
notes: Implemented /mgstudio-engine/examples/3d/tonemapping as Adapted parity for bevy/examples/3d/tonemapping.rs. Preserved interaction model: Q/W/E scene toggle, 1-6 tonemapping method switch plus Y/U proxies for methods 7/8 (current KeyCode has no Digit7/Digit8), arrow-based color grading parameter selection and adjustment, Space reset defaults, Enter recommendation reset on basic scene, H hide UI. Bevy-native tonemapping/color-grading/HDR drag-drop/custom shader path is approximated via material-space tone mapping proxies, fog/ambient response, and procedural scenes (image viewer uses bundled logo texture; HDR/EXR drag-drop is explicitly unavailable).
close_reason: Tonemapping adapted example implemented and validated

title: Port bevy/examples/3d/transmission.rs
description: Migrate and verify this Bevy example under the 0.19.0-dev vendored baseline. Record parity note with status and runtime constraints.
notes: Implemented /mgstudio-engine/examples/3d/transmission as Adapted parity for bevy/examples/3d/transmission.rs. Preserved core controls (J/K/L/. quality, O/P steps, 1/2 Q/W A/S Z/X E/R U/I parameter tuning, arrow camera, C randomize, H/D/T toggles). Semicolon key is not available in current KeyCode set, so Period is used as the Ultra-quality proxy. Bevy transmission-specific pipeline (specular/diffuse transmission fields, ScreenSpaceTransmission, HDR/Bloom/DepthPrepass/TAA runtime components) is approximated using current StandardMaterial controls, alpha blending, distance-fog proxy, and state toggles.
close_reason: Transmission adapted example implemented and validated

title: Fix mgstudio gen ECS tag detection with moon.pkg modules
description: `mgstudio gen` currently misses `#ecs.component/#ecs.resource` declarations in `moon.pkg`-based packages, which can rewrite `ecs_world/ecs_world.g.mbt` to an empty world schema. This blocks safe codegen refresh during feature work.
acceptance_criteria:
1) `mgstudio gen --check` detects existing ECS declarations in packages like render3d
2) Running `mgstudio gen` does not drop existing world component stores
3) At least one regression test/fixture covers ScreenSpaceReflections-style newly added component

title: Add CI gate for silent mgstudio gen --check
description: Add a CI guard that runs generator check against mgstudio-engine and fails if output is non-empty, so codegen drift is blocked early.
close_reason: Implemented in CI with silent codegen check script

title: Migrate mgstudio-cli build to prebuild build.js and rename main package
description: Adopt MoonBit prebuild script flow (build.js) for mgstudio-cli link config and rename CLI main package path to cmd/mgstudio so binary artifact name aligns with mgstudio.
close_reason: Implemented prebuild build.js flow and renamed CLI package to cmd/mgstudio

title: Investigate moonc link-core ICE when building mgstudio-cli release
description: moonc crashes during release build of mgstudio-cli at link-core stage with Key_not_found; investigate compiler/toolchain interactions and define a stable local reproduction + workaround.

title: Shader asset layout: separate engine/runtime builtin shaders from game shaders
description:
Problem:
- We currently store backend-specific builtin shaders under mgstudio-engine/assets/shaders/runtime_{web,native}/ and runtimes hardcode loading shaders/runtime_{web,native}/*.wgsl.
- This makes shaders look runtime-specific, and it paper-cuts engine-led rendering parity: Web and Native bring-up pipelines diverge and require shader forks.

Target outcome:
- Treat all shaders as regular assets loaded from assets-source.
- Engine owns the stable shader + bind group/buffer layout conventions (Bevy-aligned); runtime implements the host WebGPU backend without introducing shader forks.
- Define stable paths for engine builtin shaders under shaders/mgstudio/... (e.g. shaders/mgstudio/2d/sprite.wgsl).

Notes:
- If Web and Native need different WGSL, treat that as a bring-up regression and fix the runtime/pipeline implementation instead.
acceptance_criteria:
- Engine builtin shaders are treated as assets and loaded via assets-source (no runtime-owned shader directories).
- No runtime code refers to paths under shaders/runtime_web/* or shaders/runtime_native/*.
- A single WGSL implementation is used across Web + Native for sprite/mesh/gizmo pipelines (differences are fixed in bring-up, not via shader forks).
- Asset paths use a stable convention under shaders/mgstudio/...
close_reason: Implemented canonical shader assets under shaders/mgstudio and updated Web+Native runtimes to load them (no runtime_* shader paths).

title: Native mesh pipeline: add alpha blending + surface-format-aware pipeline descriptors for pos2
description:
Problem:
- Native runtime mesh pipeline currently uses a pos2-only pipeline descriptor that does not enable alpha blending and may not respect the configured surface format.

Goal:
- Provide a wgpu pipeline descriptor (or helper) for pos2 vertex buffers with alpha blending and explicit color format (similar to Web runtime).

Notes:
- Prefer solving this upstream in Milky2018/wgpu_mbt (e.g. a new desc_builders helper like render_pipeline_descriptor_color_format_alpha_blend_pos2_new), then consume it here.
- Do not vendor dependencies.
notes: Blocked on upstream Milky2018/wgpu_mbt: need a format-aware + alpha-blended pos2 render pipeline descriptor helper that works with MoonBit native stub include paths (no vendoring). Once available, wire it into mgstudio-runtime/native mesh pipeline.
close_reason: Implemented native mesh pipeline via descriptor builder: pos2 vertex layout + alpha blend + surface-format-aware pipeline selection
